claim_status: no-claim fresh-host reproduction inventory only; no independent fresh-host reproduction, no owner-approved clean-VM equivalent, no owner-reviewed fresh-host readiness, no PB-009 readiness promotion, no broad implementation readiness, no preview readiness, no beta readiness, no stable readiness, no production readiness, no release confidence, no implementation claim, and no Chrome-class claim
current_local_baseline: M0 has bootstrap, doctor, check, and xtask commands in the repository.
The wrappers set CARGO_TARGET_DIR outside the repository when the caller does not provide one.
The aggregate check validates documentation, registries, formatting, clippy, tests, shell self-test, and the architecture prototype.
The checked run-record template defines how future independent fresh-host evidence must retain command logs, hashes, cache roots, target directories, source-tree status, and failure classifications.
The checked fresh-host readiness-review template defines how future owner review must accept or reject retained run evidence before PB-009 readiness promotion.
Current-host or same-host reruns are useful diagnostics but are not independent fresh-host reproduction evidence.

host_scope: independent fresh reference host or owner-approved clean VM equivalent
exact operating system edition, version, architecture, patch level, shell, terminal, locale, timezone, CPU, memory, disk, virtualization state, network posture, and privilege level
explicit statement that no repository build outputs, Cargo target directory, Rustup toolchain cache, package cache, or generated files were reused from prior Turing work
owner-approved waiver with expiry if a clean VM is used instead of a physically separate host
source_scope: clean repository checkout at an exact Git commit with branch, remote URL, commit hash, and dirty-state output
source-tree cleanliness before and after bootstrap, doctor, check, and xtask commands
explicit list of ignored build outputs and generated caches that stay outside durable source
recorded line-ending and Git configuration facts that affect Windows, macOS, or Linux checkouts
command_scope: tools/bootstrap.sh or tools/bootstrap.ps1 output with date, shell, exit code, and environment summary
tools/doctor.sh or tools/doctor.ps1 output with date, shell, exit code, exact Rust version, rustup component state, cargo version, git version, and required host tools
tools/check.sh or tools/check.ps1 output with date, shell, exit code, aggregate validator output, cargo fmt, clippy, workspace tests, shell self-test, and prototype run
direct cargo run --locked -p xtask -- bootstrap, doctor --ci, and check output or a recorded reason that wrapper execution is the owner-approved equivalent
artifact_scope: CARGO_TARGET_DIR path, cache root path, temp directory path, and confirmation that build output stayed outside the repository
hashes or retained logs for bootstrap, doctor, check, and xtask command output
source-tree status after all commands plus evidence that no tool installer, temporary archive, credential, local profile, or generated junk was committed
failure logs and rollback notes if any command fails, including whether the failure is environmental, source-controlled, network-dependent, or nondeterministic

evidence_axes.name: Host Identity And Isolation
evidence_axes.required_output: A dated host record proving an independent fresh reference host or owner-approved clean VM equivalent with OS, shell, CPU, memory, disk, locale, timezone, network, privilege, cache, and prior-state facts.
evidence_axes.missing_status: No independent fresh-host or clean-VM run artifact exists.

evidence_axes.name: Source Checkout Identity
evidence_axes.required_output: A clean checkout record with remote URL, branch, commit hash, dirty-state output, line-ending behavior, ignored-output policy, and before/after source-tree cleanliness.
evidence_axes.missing_status: Current repository state is useful, but it is not a fresh checkout reproduction artifact.

evidence_axes.name: Bootstrap Doctor Check Output
evidence_axes.required_output: Bootstrap, doctor, check, and xtask command logs with dates, shell, exit codes, exact Rust/Cargo/Git facts, aggregate validator output, formatting, clippy, tests, shell self-test, and prototype output.
evidence_axes.missing_status: Only ordinary local command runs exist until fresh-host logs are captured.

evidence_axes.name: Cache And Target Directory Behavior
evidence_axes.required_output: Evidence for CARGO_TARGET_DIR, Cargo/Rustup cache roots, temp directories, source-tree exclusion, cleanup behavior, and no committed tool installers or generated build outputs.
evidence_axes.missing_status: Wrapper policy exists, but clean-host cache and target behavior has not been independently recorded.

evidence_axes.name: Failure Classification And Rollback
evidence_axes.required_output: If a command fails, retained logs classify the failure as environmental, source-controlled, network-dependent, or nondeterministic and record rollback or cleanup actions.

rejection_rules: Reject same-host reruns as independent fresh-host reproduction.
Reject runs that reuse prior Turing target directories, generated output, Cargo target caches, local profiles, or downloaded installers without an owner-approved clean-VM waiver.
Reject output that hides command failures, omits exit codes, omits source-tree status, or leaves build artifacts inside durable source.

Reject any claim that PB-009 is ready, broadly reproducible, preview-ready, beta-ready, stable-ready, production-ready, release-confident, implementation-ready, or Chrome-class from this inventory or the checked no-claim readiness-review template alone.
